findings.problem: Chapter 04 weaponizes a fire extinguisher even though the continuity sheet tracks the fire extinguisher as a later escalation object introduced and first weaponized in chapter_07. Pulling one from the wall here, raising it in the fight, and leaving it blood-spotted on the tile creates an object chronology contradiction and weakens the later escalation beat.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the weapon handling at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:125, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:141, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:151 so Raul's group carries and abandons only tools that are continuity-neutral in this chapter, such as the already-present pipe or pry bar, or otherwise remove the fire-extinguisher combat beat entirely so chapter_07 remains the first fire-extinguisher weaponization.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:125, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:141, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:151 no longer depict a fire extinguisher as a carried, raised, or blood-marked weapon in chapter_04. Fail if any fire-extinguisher combat beat remains on the page.

findings.problem: The first-floor clash is the chapter's major tactical turn, but the operative objective and resistance chain stay blurry on the page. Noor can only infer Raul was headed for 'the server room, the loading dock, somewhere with hardware,' and the scene leans on a later summary to tell the reader the dead man was stopping a computer-destruction attempt. Because this confrontation hinges on technical action around the dead man's switch, the local cause-and-effect is under-rendered.
findings.rewrite_direction: Clarify the assault logic directly inside snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:123, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:125, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:131, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:145: name which room or hardware Raul is trying to reach or damage, why these three people are physically blocking that corridor right now, and what specific move would trigger the switch or change everyone's survival odds before the summary paragraph at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:151 arrives.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold reader can finish snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:123 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:151 and answer four questions without inference from later chapters: what Raul's group is trying to reach or damage, why the blockers are stationed there, what action turns the standoff lethal, and how that action relates to the dead man's switch. Fail if any of those four points still has to be guessed.

findings.problem: Viktor's opening speech reads as a near-paraphrase of a calibration-only style-bible example: the same proposal frame, the same 'not because the work matters anymore' pivot, and the same argument that structure will get them through. On a chapter-defining beat, that damages voice integrity and makes the line feel derived rather than spoken fresh from this scene.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the meeting-opening speech at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:11 so Viktor still sounds committee-trained, procedural, and stressed, but does not reuse the calibration example's sentence contour or synonym chain. Give him fresh institutional vocabulary, his own qualifying sprawl, and a distinct turn into the watch-rotation idea.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised speech at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:11 no longer tracks the calibration line's 'proposal / not because the work matters anymore / structure gets us through' shape or a close synonym version of it, while still sounding recognizably like Viktor and using at least one contraction consistent with his stressed moderate-contraction register. Fail if the line still reads as a near-paraphrase of the example.

findings.problem: After Yuki's body and the corridor killing land in concrete images, the narration keeps translating them into thesis language instead of letting the new pressure stand. 'That's the new thing,' the four-deaths taxonomy, and the closing 'truth turned out to be a solvent' explanation all re-prove meanings the scene has already made legible, which thins dread into commentary.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:109, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:151, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:171, replace the taxonomic summary with new on-page consequence: a failed search, a changed alignment, bodily residue, a specific accusation, or another concrete pressure move. Keep Noor's analytic lens, but make the analysis arise from what she notices rather than from numbered classifications or thesis phrasing that culminates again at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:175.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the paragraphs beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:109, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:151, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:171 each add a new physical, social, or tactical change on the page and do not classify the deaths by type or order or restate Ember's faction-breaking strategy in abstract terms. Fail if the revision still contains summary beats equivalent to 'That's the new thing,' 'The first was...,' or 'the truth turned out to be a solvent.'

findings.problem: The chapter repeatedly leans on the same explanatory scaffold - a quality or posture defined as belonging to 'someone who...' - until it starts reading like autopilot rather than observation. The repetition is marked enough to flatten several otherwise good character beats into one reusable sentence template.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary or replace the repeated scaffold at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:49, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:127, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:129, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:137, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:165. Keep at most one of these constructions if needed; let the other beats land through concrete gesture, rhythm, or sensory detail instead of explanatory template language.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, across snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:49, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:127, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:129, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:137, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:165, no more than one 'someone who...' explanatory scaffold remains and at least three of those beats now render character state through observable action or concrete description. Fail if the repeated template still appears in multiple cited locations.

summary: Chapter 04 lands the faction break, the anonymous-stabbing escalation, and Lena's inventorying as a strong late threat, but it does not pass review. A continuity break, a copied-calibration opening speech, and repeated explanatory prose blunt the chapter's best material, and the night confrontation still withholds more tactical clarity than this turn can afford.
